name: Self-powered arsenic sensor (ArsR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: A self-powered water arsenic sensor. The native cyanobacterial ArsR regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in photosynthetic Synechocystis — which runs on light and CO2, so it can be deployed long-term without added nutrients.

input.operatingRange: ArsR responds around the 10 µg/L WHO limit and above

sensing.sensorMechanism: Arsenite binds the cyanobacterial ArsR repressor, de-repressing the ars promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: As(III) -> ArsR releases Pars -> anti-reporter sgRNA -> CRISPRi inverts a constitutive reporter (NOT). Pair an inverter for turn-on. Powered by photosynthesis.

parts.name: sgRNA
parts.source: designed against / upstream of the reporter promoter

performance.notes: Cyanobacterial ArsR arsenic sensing and Synechocystis CRISPRi are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. Photoautotroph enables self-powered, long-term deployment.

safety.biocontainment: Light/CO2-dependent growth; add inducible-essential containment for outdoor use.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check Synechocystis 6803 off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform Synechocystis 6803
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a arsenic standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native cyanobacterial ArsR arsenic sensor with Synechocystis CRISPRi
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
